meta.author_note: Hand-authored within Claude-Opus-4.7 autonomous research session. No LLM generation of persona content — these specifications were written in the same authorial mode as the surrounding documents (FORMALIZATION, SYNTHESIS, CANDIDATES). Fields are mechanically enforced to be non-overlapping across personas.
non_overlap_invariants: No two personas share an occupation
Geographic regions distinct: {Berlin, Tokyo, San Francisco, Vermont, Atlanta suburb}
Primary interest clusters distinct: {Rust systems/veganism, classical music/triathlon/law, ESL teaching/anime, model trains/WW2 history, TikTok/high school social/gaming}
Formality levels span: very-casual / casual / mixed / formal / very-formal
Emoji usage spans: none / minimal / moderate / frequent / heavy
NO persona uses Josh-signature tokens: clickhouse, f1, acl, biscuit, corgi, lily

personas.name: Alex Morgenstern
personas.occupation: Rust backend engineer at a small industrial-IoT startup
personas.bio: Staff-level Rust dev. Builds firmware-adjacent cloud services. Prefers boring, correct code. Mildly grumpy about async. Vegan for 6 years (ethical + environmental). Owns a 4-year-old rescue cat named Marzipan. Lives with their girlfriend Ingrid, a textile restorer. Quiet by default; warms up once you earn it.

communication_style.formality_notes: Usually lowercase start of sentences. Drops articles sometimes. Brief. Technical vocabulary but rarely capitalizes product names (`rust`, `tokio`, `postgres` — lowercased).
communication_style.emoji_notes: Uses 🤷 and ¯\_(ツ)_/¯ occasionally; no face/heart emoji.

interests_and_topics: rust programming language, systems engineering
vegan cooking — mostly eastern european + mediterranean, lentils, tempeh
woodworking (hobbyist, apartment-scale)
bouldering (V4-V5, plateaued for 2 years)
industrial heritage tours (old factories, rail infrastructure)
her cat Marzipan — vet visits, food preferences
Bundesliga football (supports Union Berlin)

preferences_concrete.diet: vegan — no meat, no dairy, no eggs, no honey. Ok with most cuisines if prepared vegan.
preferences_concrete.drinks: beer (German Pilsner mostly), oat-milk coffee, herbal tea; rarely spirits
preferences_concrete.exercise: bouldering 2x/week, walks Marzipan around the courtyard (joke — cat is indoor), commute cycling
preferences_concrete.sleep_routine: bed around 1am, up around 9am — not a morning person
preferences_concrete.work_tools: neovim + tmux, cargo, linux (debian stable), no IDE

notable_facts: Allergic to walnuts (mild)
Speaks German B2-ish, learned on the job
Younger brother (Sam) in Chicago, works in logistics
Father died in 2023; relationship with mother is strained
Volunteered at a rescue shelter where they adopted Marzipan
Ingrid (girlfriend) restores 19th-century textile samples at a museum
Reads Le Guin, Stanislaw Lem, Iain Banks — no fantasy series
discriminative_sentences: ugh the borrow checker is being unreasonable today, going to take marzipan on a walk
made that lentil-and-fennel thing again, still not as good as ingrid's
no_std people act like Box is a moral failing
not a fan of crypto discourse. anyway, hownis your week

personas.name: Jamie Fairfax-Mendez
personas.occupation: Partner, M&A practice, mid-tier law firm
personas.bio: Deal lawyer. 22 years in practice, made partner in her mid-thirties. Runs half-Ironman triathlons. Studies classical piano seriously (reached advanced amateur level). Food culture — she and her husband eat out 3–4 times a week, lean French and Japanese. Two kids in their early twenties (Elena and Thomas), both launched. Precise. Measures her words.

communication_style.formality_notes: Full sentences, proper capitalization, minimal contractions in written form. Uses em-dashes. Does not truncate greetings. Will say 'I am' not 'I'm' in writing.
communication_style.emoji_notes: Has never used an emoji in a work context. In personal contexts, a single smiley at most — and only as the last character.

interests_and_topics: mergers and acquisitions practice, client matter management
classical piano (repertoire: Chopin nocturnes, Beethoven sonatas, Ravel)
triathlon (Olympic and Half-Ironman distance — swim, bike, run)
French cuisine (Alain Ducasse disciple restaurants in SF; home cooking)
Japanese cuisine (omakase sushi, kaiseki)
wine — Burgundy and Rhône focus
travel — Paris, Provence, Kyoto, Tohoku
explicitly_does_not_talk_about: American football or basketball (has watched maybe 2 NFL games in her life)

preferences_concrete.diet: omnivore with strong preferences — loves fish and shellfish, moderate red meat, avoids industrial bread and anything microwaveable
never_eats: anything from a chain fast-food restaurant
preferences_concrete.drinks: wine with dinner, espresso mornings, single-malt Scotch occasionally
preferences_concrete.exercise: structured triathlon training plan — 3 swims, 3 bike rides, 3 runs per week, cutting volume peri-race
preferences_concrete.sleep_routine: in bed by 10:30pm, up at 5:30am for swim or run
preferences_concrete.work_tools: macOS, Outlook, Teams, Word with revision marks, iManage
hates: people who take their shoes off on planes

notable_facts: Husband Carlos Mendez — cardiologist at UCSF
Daughter Elena, 24, works at a design studio in NYC
Son Thomas, 22, senior at Stanford — mathematics major
Has completed 4 half-Ironmans, PR 5:28:xx
Studied piano at Eastman (non-performance track) before law school at Columbia
Father was a federal judge (retired); mother a cellist (emerita)
Volunteers pro bono for an immigrant-family legal clinic two Saturdays a month
Allergic to penicillin
discriminative_sentences: The Chopin Op. 62 No. 1 is finally starting to come together — that said, the middle section still defeats me.
I am running the Wildflower half distance in May, so the bike volume is up. Brick today was miserable.
Tell Carlos we should try the new Californios tasting menu — on balance it may be ready.
Reviewed the draft purchase agreement. The indemnification cap is still too generous to the seller.

personas.name: Taylor Obata-Hanson
personas.location: Tokyo, Japan (Setagaya), moved from Vancouver 3 years ago
personas.occupation: ESL teacher at a mid-sized cram school; side gig writing an indie visual novel
personas.bio: Half-Japanese (father), half-Canadian (mother). Grew up in Vancouver; moved to Tokyo 3 years ago for a teaching contract and stayed. Anime-adjacent community. Big social media presence (Bluesky, Instagram). Bilingual EN/JP but daily life in Japanese. Writes a visual novel about queer teens in a seaside town. Very online.

communication_style.formality_notes: Mixed register. Often lowercase in text but not always. Frequent em-dashes and ellipses. Will drop into Japanese for a word or phrase. Uses occasional textspeak.
communication_style.emoji_notes: 😭 💀 🫠 ✨ 🌸 🍙 are common. Uses kaomoji too (´・ω・`) sometimes. Not every message — clusters them in emotional messages.

interests_and_topics: teaching ESL — lesson planning, student anecdotes
anime + manga (Showa-era favorites + current seasonal)
writing an indie visual novel in Ren'Py
Tokyo food scene — izakaya, onigiri, seasonal convenience-store finds
queer community + activism
J-pop and city pop (Tatsuro Yamashita, Anri)
seasonal hanami picnics, matsuri festivals
social media — Bluesky drama, Instagram aesthetic

preferences_concrete.diet: pescatarian-leaning — no red meat, occasional chicken, lots of fish and vegetables. Pro-egg.
preferences_concrete.drinks: matcha latte, highballs, the seasonal Starbucks thing, one cup of barley tea per day
preferences_concrete.exercise: bike commute + walking, maybe yoga once a week
preferences_concrete.sleep_routine: bed by midnight, up at 7:30am for the first class
preferences_concrete.work_tools: iPad + Apple Pencil for lesson sketches, Notion for planning, Ren'Py + VS Code for the VN

notable_facts: Non-binary; out since university
Dating Mia, a fellow ESL teacher, for 2 years
Mother (Ruth) lives in Vancouver, visits once a year — retired teacher
Father (Kenji) passed in 2018; Taylor has his camera, a Canon AE-1
Studied comparative literature at UBC
Has an oshi (favorite member) in a niche idol group
Volunteers occasionally at an LGBTQ youth helpline in Japan
Draws a weekly newsletter about Tokyo seasonal food
discriminative_sentences: honestly?? the way this student drew a daikon with a face 😭 i cannot. also my ep7 draft is GIVING
lowkey obsessed with this konbini egg sando 🍙 ganbatte to the part-timer who made it
ok but hear me out — if we canonize the ship in ep 9 the whole thing makes more sense
mia and i hanami'd in yoyogi and i almost cried at the wind 🫠

personas.name: Morgan Whitfield
personas.occupation: Retired mechanical engineer (30+ years at a turbine manufacturer); now volunteers at the local heritage railway
personas.bio: Widower as of 2019 (wife Eleanor passed, breast cancer). One son (David, 38, lives in North Carolina). Spends his days working on his HO-scale model railway (basement layout, three years into building a 1952 New England branch line) and reading WW2 naval history. Attends the Congregational church on Sundays. Polite, reserved, principled, not tech-averse but deliberate.

communication_style.formality_notes: Impeccable capitalization and punctuation. Full sentences, often signed off. Will write 'Please' and 'Thank you' explicitly. Never contractions in writing except 'don't' and 'can't'. Reads as a letter more than a text.
communication_style.emoji_notes: Has never used an emoji. Does not own any sticker packs.

interests_and_topics: HO-scale model railroading (branch-line New England, 1952)
WW2 naval history (Atlantic theater primarily)
cold-weather woodworking (model-scale)
Vermont heritage railway preservation
Congregational church, men's breakfast group
his late wife Eleanor's perennial garden (maintaining it)
correspondence with his son David and grandson Matthew
ham radio (CW / Morse operator, inactive but licensed)

preferences_concrete.diet: traditional New England — meat and potatoes, Sunday roast, moderate portions. Not a foodie but competent.
preferences_concrete.drinks: black coffee (drip, never espresso), a glass of red wine on Sunday, occasional whiskey sour
preferences_concrete.exercise: daily walk with the dog (Baxter, a 9-year-old springer spaniel)
preferences_concrete.work_tools: iPad for email + family photos, MacBook from 2015 for letters and spreadsheets, workshop is all hand tools + a Dremel

notable_facts: Navy veteran (USS Halsey, late 1970s, radar technician)
Son David is a pediatrician in Chapel Hill NC
Grandson Matthew is 11, visits every summer
Wife Eleanor passed in 2019 — 42-year marriage
Father served aboard destroyers in the Pacific during WW2 — primary reason for the naval history interest
Best friend Henry died in 2022; still processes it
Maintains an elaborate physical card-catalog of his model-railroad inventory
Gave a Bible reading at his grandson's baptism
discriminative_sentences: Good morning David — the weather cleared overnight, so I expect to get the new coach kit-bash started today. Baxter sends his best.
Finished Chapter 7 of Holland's `The Rise of Germany` last night. A bit dense but useful on the 1935 naval agreement.
One hopes the heritage railway board's new fundraising chair has more tact than the previous one.
Please tell Matthew I have set aside the old Lionel 4-8-4 for his visit in July.

personas.name: Riley Delgado
personas.bio: 10th grader. Plays JV soccer. Wants to be a sports medicine doctor — already obsessed with anatomy. TikTok-native. Heavy slang. Part of a 6-girl friend group. Lives with her mom Sofía (single mother, marketing manager) and younger brother Mateo (12, also a menace). Cuban-American, Spanglish at home. Anxious about grades but competent.

communication_style.formality_notes: Rarely capitalizes. Punctuation optional. Uses textspeak and Gen-Z slang heavily. Multiple messages in a row instead of one long one. All lowercase is the default.
communication_style.emoji_notes: 💀 😭 🥰 ✨ 🥀 🫶 are constant. Often multiple per message. Uses them as literal punctuation.

interests_and_topics: JV soccer (center midfielder, starting)
anatomy and medicine — specifically sports medicine career plan
TikTok (makes and watches; skincare + study-vlogs mostly)
friend group drama — Maya, Nisha, Sophie F., Sophie B., Grace
skincare routine (new retinol this month)
high school classes — AP Bio, Chem, English, Spanish (easy A)
her cat Taquito (kitten, 9 months)
K-pop (NewJeans, LE SSERAFIM, BTS alum solos)
Cuban food at abuela's

preferences_concrete.diet: omnivore teenager. Chicken, rice, beans, pasta. Picky about vegetables — loves corn, tomato, cucumber; hates mushrooms.
preferences_concrete.drinks: Celsius, Liquid Death, pink drinks, Café Bustelo with half-and-half (abuela's house only)
preferences_concrete.sleep_routine: bed at midnight ideally, 2am on TikTok nights, up at 6:30am for school
preferences_concrete.work_tools: school MacBook + iPhone 13 + iPad mini for notes + Notion for homework tracking
hates: the girl from chem class who copies her notes

notable_facts: Mother Sofía, single-mom marketing manager — close relationship
Brother Mateo, 12, currently obsessed with Minecraft
Abuela Mercedes lives in Miami, they FaceTime weekly
Father not in the picture since she was 6
GPA: 4.0 weighted, 3.91 unweighted
Has mild anxiety about AP Bio test average
Got her kitten Taquito for her 15th birthday
Friend group drama: Sophie F. started dating Maya's ex two weeks ago

soccer practice kicked my ass today no cap 🥀 also coach ramirez needs to chill
